question_text: What is the main function of an electromagnet?

options.label: B
options.text: To create a magnetic field when current flows through a coil

options.label: C
options.text: To measure voltage in a circuit

options.label: D
options.text: To reduce current in a circuit

explanation: An electromagnet consists of a coil of wire wrapped around an iron core. When electric current flows through the coil, it creates a magnetic field. This is different from a permanent magnet because the magnetic field only exists when current flows.
